rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORRAINE D. ELLIOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/14/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) The proof of service filed on 6/25/24 did not show that an AMENDED petition had been filed and would be heard. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number shows an unlikely birthdate for the petitioner. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 8/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVA SMALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues raised by the Court at the previous hearings have not been addressed: 1) No FINAL inventory and appraisal has been filed since the administrator received all assets from the decedent's conservator more than 18 months ago. 2) One partial inventory and appraisal was filed in January 2024, but it only lists real property (but not cash and tangible personal items identified in the conservatorship final accounting as date-of-death assets). 3) The administrator's current bond ($1,197,000) is insufficient, based on the partial inventory and appraisal value ($1,470,000) alone, but no ex parte petition has been filed to increase the bond. The Court is to consider whether the administrator should be suspended and/or whether statutory fee reduction under Probate Code section 12205 may be warranted at the hearing for final distribution or allowance of fees. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHARLES WOHLETZ IRREVOCABLE SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST, dated march 22, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Removing Chris Zampach As Successor Trustee And Appointing Armand Russo As Successor Trstee Of The Charles Wohletz Irrevocable Special Needs Trust Dated March 22, 2001
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Court to determine whether jurisdiction and venue are proper here. Note: It is unclear whether the allegation is that there is no trustee or that Chris Zampach is the currently acting trustee who should be removed. 2) Petitioner requests removal of Judy Spain as trustee, but she is no longer the trustee due to her death. 3) Petitioner requests authority of the proposed successor trustee to designate future trustees and to designate a different contingent beneficiary; however, this would modify the trust terms, and no request to modify trust has been made. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EVELINE S. WALKER INTER VIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee To Fill Vacancy
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the petition should have been filed in the existing case: PTR-97-268002.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNESTINE PHILOMENA BURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 10/5/23. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 7/21/25. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 7/21/25, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 2000 WALLACE CHIN AND CAROL LEM CHIN REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED 12/5/00
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trustee And Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/19/2024. Notes emailed to counsel on 07/03/2024.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IVAN HUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/14/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Now, an amended petition has been filed. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends, including publication. [CRC 7.53(a).] Proof of publication of Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate must be filed. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DUANE LEE FRISBIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Off calendar at counsel's request.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACOB TICE POWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/14/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The will is not self-proving. A Proof of Subscribing Witness must be filed. (The witnesses' signature page was also incomplete re the number of pages.) (2) The attorney's signature on the petition is also incompletely dated. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The original will was just filed on 6/27/24, so it has not yet been provided to the assigned examiner.)
